body.title: igor launcher — start in named tmux session
body.result: Backfill only — impl shipped in ea0cba88. No code changes needed. Closed by CC.
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
Running igor outside tmux starts the process detached from any session, so a terminal close or disconnect kills the restart loop mid-run. The fix steals the pattern from superclaude: if already inside tmux, exec directly; otherwise create/reuse a session named igor and attach. Controlled by IGOR_TMUX_SESSION env var (default igor).

Already implemented and committed in ea0cba88. This ticket is a traceability backfill.

Affected files:
- TheIgors/igor (15-line addition before the final exec)

Design rules:
- postgres-only: not applicable — no data persistence changes introduced. Existing log writes (utility_closet.log) are pre-existing and unchanged.
- oop-first: not applicable — pure shell script, no Python classes.
- docs-in-code: not applicable — shell launcher, behavior is self-documenting.
- no-new-memory-schemas: not applicable.
- test-plan-or-why-not: manual only — pure shell launcher behavior, no Python logic changed. No automated test needed.

Scope boundary:
IN: TheIgors/igor launcher script — tmux session wrapping.
OUT: installer.py, worker_foreman, restart loop logic (all unchanged).

Test plan:
- Manual: tmux kill-session -t igor 2>/dev/null; igor — creates session and attaches.
- Manual: start igor from inside tmux — does not create a nested session.
- Exit-42 restart loop re-invokes igor from inside tmux, so the [[ -n TMUX ]] branch fires correctly — no double-wrap.
